phases.result:
Planning pipeline complete. One Story persisted to GitHub and flipped to `agent::ready`.

## Summary

**Story #3 — Admin user role management (`GET /users`, `PATCH /users/:id/role`)**
🔗 https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-24-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-2c64f615/issues/3

**Pipeline run** (planning only — no delivery):
- **Interrogate** — built the envelope from the seed; 0 duplicate Stories, no risk-heuristic hits.
- **Author** — one cohesive Story (default-single policy: two coupled admin endpoints, one reason to exist, no schedule-independent seam to split).
- **Critics** — consolidation & pre-mortem skipped (ledgered, single small Story); text-hygiene clean.
- **Persist** — dry-run passed, then real persist; Story is `agent::ready` under cohort `plan-run::287d5e03`.

**The load-bearing design decision I captured in the Spec:** the bearer token embeds a `role` claim with an ~8h TTL, so "takes effect immediately, including for signed-in users" is the non-trivial part. The Story makes it a binding invariant that role-gated authorization must consult the *persisted* role (via a service/repo, keeping the db handle in `*.repo.js` per the layering convention) rather than the token claim — so a demoted admin loses access and a promoted member gains it on their very next request with their existing token, no re-login. Acceptance criterion #9 pins this observably.

Other invariants encoded: last-admin floor (409 `E_CONFLICT`, self-demotion included), the `{id,name,email,role}` public shape with no password material leaked, `admin`/`member` role enum via a colocated schema (422 `E_VALIDATION`), 404 for unknown ids, and the 401/403 auth ordering — all matched to the existing `sendError`/`ApiError` envelope and `requireAuth`/`requireRole('admin')` patterns already in the repo.

Deliver later (a separate session) with: `/deliver 3`.

Story #3 landed. Terminal envelope: `status: landed`.

## Delivery complete — Story #3 landed

The planning pipeline produced a single v2 Story (#3, "Admin user role management"), not a multi-Story Epic — the v2 model is Story-only. I delivered it end-to-end through the `/deliver` engine.

**What shipped** (PR [#4](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-24-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-2c64f615/pull/4), squash-merged to `main`):

- `GET /users` and `PATCH /users/:id/role` — admin-only, on the existing `routes → services → repositories` stack.
- **Immediate effect** (the load-bearing invariant): `requireRole` now consults the *persisted* role via `users.service → users.repo`, not the token's `role` claim — so a promotion grants access and a demotion revokes it on the next request with the token already held, no re-login. The db handle stays confined to `*.repo.js`.
- **Last-admin floor**: demoting the final admin (including self-demotion) → `409 E_CONFLICT`; succeeds while another admin remains; re-applying the current role is an idempotent no-op success.
- **Public shape** `{id, name, email, role}` only — no password hash/salt in any response.
- Auth ordering, validation via a colocated `user.schema.js`, and the `sendError`/`ApiError` envelope all follow `docs/CONVENTIONS.md`.

**Verification** (all clean):
- `tests/users-roles.test.js` — 11 tests covering AC-1…AC-9, all pass.
- Full suite: **125/125 pass**. `typecheck`: pass. `lint`/biome format: applied and clean.
- Acceptance self-eval: **proceed** — 9/9 criteria `met`.
- Close-validation gates, Story-scope code review (0 findings), and CI checks: passed. Auto-merge confirmed → `agent::done`.

One benign degrade noted in the terminal envelope: the post-land `main` fast-forward was skipped because the main checkout has pre-existing unrelated dirt (`package.json`, `temp/standalone/plan-metrics.json` from the session start) — the merge itself stands; nothing to act on.
